channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

stay on topic, sick fuck, who gives a flying fuck about the pic rel if it was edited or not in a chink phone or whatsoever, fucks sake, get fucked nigger mutt

shut the fuck up faggot

62125301

If she doesn't wipe thoroughly once you can smell shit when hitting from the back

I'm too autistic to lie so it cost me a gf

62125445

62125550

you did good anon, its disgusting

62125584

62125585
it's hapoooooning

62125592
>>62125584

still smells bad, just grow a pair and tell your smelly cunt to wash her shit stained ass, you mutts don't even have a bidet, imagine the smell, fuck

62125627

I never claimed to be OK with a shit stained ass retard, but even a relatively clean asshole will dilate and leak odors during intercourse, perfectly fucking normal

62125701

he fucks trannies, you just know

62125733

No, you're just an insufferable incel autistic fag whose never fucked a woman and can't understand that they are, in fact, human and not plastic dolls

lel, have fun fucking dialated asses, faggot

62125829

you enjoy smelling turds

62125858

item.title: You DID secure your moon bag…right, anon? — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
You DID secure your moon bag…right, anon?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-17 16:58 UTC

OP

You DID secure your moon bag…right, anon?

Early replies (first 3)

62125227
>>62124756
they will get turned away, even if the crowd lined up at the current levels! there will only be 21 million coins and many people have more than one already. the crowds will be turned away since there is not enough of them left. i read that there are already something like 200k bitcoin holders and only a million coins left to be mined. even if each of those new coins went to a new holder (they won't) the maximum number of people that can have a bitcoin at the same time is 1.2 million.

62125283
I placed an order last weekend and initiated an ACH transfer to buy more tomorrow.
t. lucky retard

62125298
>>62125283
>lucky retard
well, half of that statement is true.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62125310
>>62124756
It will dump when the ceasefire fails (like it always does). Hopefully you sold the fake pump… You did sell the pump, right, anon..?

62125355
>>62124756
change 67k to $500 and 1M to +50,000

62125410
>>62124756
i did buyed

62125446
>>62125310
Yes

62125582
>>62124756
It’s hilarious how dumb people are to think BTC is even worth the 20x considering btc’s product. BTC’s original use was decentralized anonymous currency and it’s become neither decentralized nor anonymous. It’s defied the very thing it set out to do while there are better coins working to become more centralized and other coins that are more anonymous. BTC only caught up once it became more centralized. You want massive banks and governments backing your crypto money laundering scheme and if you know one better than BTC, I would invest in that before BTC because it’s bound to have better margins. Bitcoin has only become a hedge for better, more lucrative coins, ironically enough.

62125583
>>62125227
>21 million coins
>21,000,000 x 100,000,000 satoshi
>2.1 quadrillion
Oh no

62125597
>>62125298
thank you

62125644
>>62124756
If that many normal people are buying bitcoin at £1M it means currency has hyper inflated so much that thats cheap and poorfags can afford it and not only that every poorfag has £1M for bitcorn something has gone wrong, Also what if the new Chinese order decide to just turn it off by decrypting the entire network and dumping the private keys for lols

62125672
>>62125310
It won't dump until July.

62125766
>>62125310
Selling is for fags. Just buy weekly and hold until it hits a million. If it fails (it clearly won’t) then oh well. You lost a gamble.

62125827
>>62125582

>it’s not decentralized
There are 70,000+ nodes worldwide enforcing bitcoins rules. Unless you think 1 guy secretly owns them all?

>its not anonymous
Good luck getting widespread adoption and ETFs for your monero

>it succeeded because it became more centralized
You have this backwards.

I wish you luck with your altcoins but your post is incoherent. It’s bad because it became more centralized while other coins are good for becoming more centralized?

CHOP CHOP HURRY UP OR GET WRITTEN UP

62119436
>>62119165

62119443
Some people have almost no power over anything in their life. They do trivial evil acts like this because its their only thing they can do. Stealing stationary from work is a similar thing. His life is probably miserable.

62119490
>>62119443
Put the fries in the bag, wagie

62119494
>>62119165

>>62119443
cope

62119504
>>62119494
You can do it at any retail store.

62119509
>>62119504
i see they have Starbucks too. i'll give a try. ill get the bros. "meet up at Target this weekend" lmao.

62119524
>>62119509
Bruh. Slap the wagie if he acts up.

62119527
>>62119165
Littering is third world street shitting behavior

62119540
>>62119527
You are dalit

62119581
>>62119540

62120633

62120744
>>62119540

62120956
>>62119165
I hate that he is fat and old.

62121003
>>62119165
Just stand next to him and fart.

62121055
I wish I had a reason to live. I wish I had a family. Why do we bother going on when it's pointless

62121091
>>62119494
>>62119504
one time I was eating food I bought at a grocery store on the table they had in front of the store and they literally called the cops to kick me off the property.
I'm not even black and I was minding my own business.

62121170
>>62119165
>Every day I look up at the stars at night and say to myself....there has to be something more than this..

62121194
>>62121003
yes let me fart on demand

62121283

62121287

62121327

62121528
>>62121283
>>62121287
>>62121327
It's incredible how low some people will sink and how much humiliation they'll eat up just to avoid being hungry.

62123792
>>62122207
I don't get it.

62123830
>>62119443
didn't ask for your opinion i did ask for a free refill and clean up this mess while you're at it wagie

62124064
>>62123792
money, glass, water, ketchup. whats going to happen if you try to take the money?

62124173
>>62124064
Oh, I thought it was something else. Guess I need to visit the optometrist.

62124245
>>62119165
it would be worth a firing to punch him

62124743

62125214

item.title: Crude Oil — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Crude Oil

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-04-17 17:00 UTC

OP

>there was some anon on this Business & Finance board, posting multiple angry posts and crashing out, while arguing in all seriousness, with 100% sincerity, that oil would go to $220
>two hundred united statesian American dollarinos
>there were multiple ESL foreigners posting in agreement, in fact the majority of the thread was anons crashing out

My fucking sides are in orbit. Panicans absolutely blown the FUCK out once again! NEVER gets old!

Early replies (first 3)

62125480
they imploded futures with direct treasury manipulation
the trade got crowded and volatile
the result was inevitable

some people trade without thinking that others also have access to the info they just read online
they don't think "my neighbor could be thinking the same thing"
so they crowd the same sale

62125514
>>62125480
Reeks of cope

/biz/ is just full of retarded brown foreigners, at the end of the day. Very important to keep this in mind when taking any advice from this board. The smartest posters made money and left.

62125548
>>62125514
oil is being phased out
not even by green energy
look at how much GW the United States is building in NatGas power plants to fuel data centers

this was a short term trade
anyone thinking about second, third, forth order effects are completely out of the loop
even the Iraq war made oil spike through a year and resumed dumping after a year
and that had boots on the ground

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62125572
>>62125548
>"Oil is going to $200"
>No it's not, you're a retard
>oil dumps to $80

>"Rather than admitting I was wrong and that I am a retard, here's a bunch of meaningless drivel about why I was actually correct (even though I was objectively wrong)

I don't care about your cope, dude. Oil was never going to $200 and a bunch of retarded brown foreigners were posting multiple threads about how oil was going to go ballistic and how obvious it was that oil was going to $200, etc.

You were wrong, and that was obvious from the beginning. Deal with it

62125593
the issue as usual is Trump being a nigger lying about shit to pump the markets

62125601
>>62125572
>Rather than admitting I was wrong
but I didn't trade oil
I was agreeing with you, little bro, go read my posts again lol
chill out

62125799
>>62125548
>oil is being phased out
that's the fate of all non-renewables. because they run out

62125819
You retards larping as humans has me seriously considering buying oil before market close.
It is a Friday after all.
In the middle of the economic part of WW3.
What did Mr orange pedophile say again?
Yeah, do the opposite.

62125837
>>62125593
What will be the next fud to turbodump the markets? Like, that October 10th tariff announcemen tlevel?

item.title: Let's All Get Rich Together — 18 replies — 33.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Let's All Get Rich Together

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-04-17 16:35 UTC

OP

>Gold: World's biggest asset, now worth $33 trillion
>Bitcoin: Was in the top 10 of all assets, peak of $2.5 trillion

And yet nothing happened.

Why?

Because neither of these assets threaten the dollar, or any fiat currency (total market cap of all currencies is about twice that of gold now at its peak)

The plan: We all buy and hold nano (XNO), causing the price to rise and associated interest to grow, with a push for adoption as a currency.

>Why not [my shitcoin] instead of nano?
Nano is the apex currency, both in performance (feeless; fastest settlement ~350ms; lowest energy cost per transaction) and ideologically (fully distributed by the founder for free; no inflation).

It has been around for over a decade and only improved over time, a functional, decentralised, cryptocurrency. If you don't believe me, ask an AI model.

>Won't we get dumped on by each other?
People will sell, but so long as the idea survives, the potential marketcap is still deep in the trillions.

Nothing is more powerful than an idea.

Early replies (first 3)

62118570
HOLY SHIT

By sheer chance I happened to be watching Sunny today, "The gang finds a dumpster baby", and recognised one of the frames Asslarper used (pic related).

In this episode, Dennis (AB's avatar) convinces an activist to CHAINLINK himself to a tree, then LITERALLY cuckolds him.

>A SINGULAR TREE
>THE cuckOLDS OF CRYPTO!

Now do you believe me?

Inverse Assblaster; what did he tell you was useless?

62118587
Wait a minute ... those are the exact same arguments ...

are you saying that ... ?

no ...

62118619
nigga just short oil
let the goyim deal in the real world

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62118645
>>62118570
Kek somebody finally got the joke

62118653
>>62118619
>he thinks middle Eastern wars for Israel just end
you must be 18 to post here

62118695
>>62118568
kek

62118861
>>62118568
>>62118570
>>62118587
>>62118695
>>62118653
>>62118645
>>62118619
>>62118587
>We all buy and hold nano (XNO)
Reddit already tried this. Go back

62118881
>>62118861
Let's finish the fight.

62119564
>>62118568
Holy shit this pathetic broke loser is back.
Seriously dude just end your worthless existence. No one is ever pumping your bags you low iq ape. You were born to slave away at whatever shitty menial job you work at til the day you die.

62120046
>>62119564
Silence, matrix agent! An unemployed schizophrenic is speaking!

62122296
Ready to win, /biz/?

62122884
I said are you ready to win /biz/?

62123845
I CAAAAANN'TTT HEAAARRR YOUUUUUU??

I SAID ARE YOU READY TO WIN, /BIZ/?!

62123950
>>62123845
YES I AM
THATS WHY IM STAYING THE FUCK AWAY FROM NANOO NANOO

62123994
>>62118568
Nano? Nah, no.

62124013
>>62123950
What you don't realise is we're already at the finish line

>>62123994
Yes? Yes, YES!

62125690
Pssst, /biz/

Let's all get rich

item.title: I have been fired lol — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I have been fired lol

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-04-17 17:07 UTC

OP

After 10 years working in a big corpo. I went to my office today and the security guard was there. He didn't let me touch anything and escorted me to HHRR but I already knew what was going to happen.
They didn't give me any reasons. My boss didnt even look at my eyes. They don't want to go to court so they paid me a HUGE severance pay + TWO years of full unemployment benefits (EU country)
And now i'm just here sitting in my living room still processing that after a decade I won't need to wake up early and fight with my bosses and customers anymore. It feels weird.
I probably will take a sabbatical in SEA or something.
Holy shit. It really feels weird.
What would you do /biz/? I honestly dont care about a big gap on my resume and money is not an issue now

Early replies (first 3)

62125619
>>62125608
lol

62125630
What are you good at? Capitalize on your hobbies with seed money from the severance package.

62125698
>>62125630
>Capitalize on your hobbies
>Hiking, filming and traveling.
Oh great i'm going to be a youtuber now.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62125743
>>62125608
Sounds like a country with high taxes. What are the rules for taxes if you're outside of the country? Become a trader, open a business, etc. Get into DeFi if your country is trying to screw you on taxes.

62125760
>>62125698
I like that, in death, all this kike cared about was getting a swipe in at another jewtube kike. What a fuckin loser lmao

62125804
>>62125608
Where's the party, anon?

62125813
>>62125608
>HUGE severance pay + TWO years of full unemployment benefits
i'm jealous

62125875
>>62125743
>Sounds like a country with high taxes
If I legally sell my BTC bag these bastards will take 23%, so yes. But no trading for me. I'm terrible at it.
>>62125804
Pattaya? Im still deciding. But today im going to eat a good steak and get drunk at home.
>>62125813
>i'm jealous
Don't be. It took a DECADE of corporate hell to get this, and piss off a regional CEO enough to not even want to go to court with their army of lawyers. They just wanted me OUT as soon as possible.
Damn, what the fuck did i do lmao.

item.title: Something is coming soon — 2 replies — 6.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Something is coming soon

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-04-17 16:49 UTC

OP

If you’re wondering where the recent FUD is coming from it’s because we’re only months away from the release of the CyberCode 2.0 testnet. As an insider, you have no idea what the importance of CbC 2.0 is going to be for LINK when the alpha releases around the end of November (give or take a few months) so I will spoonfeed you out of kindness of heart:

CyberCode 2.0 secures the ledger cryptographically to encode transactions at 20x of the legacy infrastructure. It achieves this by taking blockchain transactions to the BbC-2026 layer and parsing them through the node network incentivized by super linear sequencing (SLSeq) which Sergey said is very close to the finish line. This is all dynamically incentivized through semi-permeable staking (SPS) allowing LINK token holders to benefit for verifying data both on the main chain and the Blockchain Byzantine Consensus 2026 layer. No other project is even close to doing this in the oracle space rn. It’s not even close!

The obvious benefits of super fast transaction speeds (obSFTS) also brings exponential economies of scale. What this means is if farmer has 50 Kg pig for sale, he can put the beast to market on the blockchain to verify the weight in a trust less system faster than global SWIFT system. Soon any business who wants to stay relevant will have to buy LINK to have access to the CyberCode 2.0 feature. A truly unique proposition! Those who don’t use this system (powered by LINK) will get left behind. So get your ducks in a row. Just keep buying and NEVER sell. We are all in this together LINKbloodbros.

Scammer motherfuckers want you to think that banks and other organizations are not currently fulling their bags with cheap LINK as we speak. DON’T NOT LET THEM WIN! If you hold 50 or more LINK right now, you already made it. Immediately when we breach the $10 resistance it’ll be too late for you. You have been warned. So go buy many LINK for you dream to come true.

Early replies (first 3)

62125665
>>62125647
DR;NS

62125781
HOLY SHIT

By sheer chance I happened to be watching Sunny today, "The gang finds a dumpster baby", and recognised one of the frames Asslarper used (pic related).

In this episode, Dennis (AB's avatar) convinces an activist to CHAINLINK himself to a tree, then LITERALLY cuckolds him.

>A SINGULAR TREE
>THE cuckOLDS OF CRYPTO!

Now do you believe me?

Inverse Assblaster; what did he tell you was useless???

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: How much BTC you holding right now? — 71 replies — 75.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
How much BTC you holding right now?

Open thread • Replies: 71 • Updated: 2026-04-17 16:11 UTC

OP

How much BTC do you currently have in your wallet, and what’s your target amount to hold before it reaches $1M?

>I mean, if you don’t even have at least 1 BTC and you’re in crypto, it still feels pointless.

Early replies (first 3)

62114988
>>62114930
1.03

ill keep buying as pay cheques come in as long as we stay around the 4 year SMA

ill stop buying when we start running up and instead start selling to buy other assets, probably pay off my mortgage

62115186
>>62114930
i cope with my 5 stack and tell myself in 10 years i'll make it.

62115188
>>62114930
350 BTC or so.

Feels good. I'm living pretty modestly in a $2MM 5000 square foot house in a low cost state. Probably going to build a home gym extension with a sauna and hyperbaric oxygen chamber. Also a small fallout shelter just to be comfy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62120210
>>62117820
what's farming yield

62120218
>>62114930
Not enough

62120220
>>62120218
Make it enough

62120223
>>62120200
Just secure your btc in safe place

62121262
>>62115503
What else are you holding?

62121409
>>62114930
Only 1. It's not enough sadly

62121442
>>62119498
A bitcoin ETF on the American stock market.

62121496
>>62114930
I have 1 BTC spot and like 3 or 4 worth in ETFs in my IRA. I don't buy spot BTC at all and buy alts instead because I'm a masochist and want to feel like kys'ing myself everyday until I finally giga/makeit/ in altszn. I will probably hodl my 1 bitshit forever and sell everything else in the end.

62122124
>>62117820
Would be great to see TBVs go live soon. Could really open up more BTC use cases beyond just holding.

62122129
>>62115773
It's kinda enough , you can increase little by little.

62122136
>>62116578
tomorrow, don't worry about it lol

62122141
People are still buying the Epstein Pedo coin? Oof, brutal

62123324
>>62116632
Yeah you can

62123590
>>62122124
I'm not convinced of bitcoin usecases. It's just another promising word spoken

62123601
>>62121442
That's centralized shit

62123609
>>62122141
Hahahaa ngmi

62123784
>>62120210
It’s part of a BTCFi concept around using BTC in DeFi without changing custody assumptions.

62123785
>>62116881
Guess it's about hardwork

62123812
>>62115489
I'll retire early with that money

62123814
>>62123784
Why do you like risking when you can hold only

62123856
>>62115773
Not enough

62123911
>>62121496
Just sell it today bobo

62124101
>>62114930
I hold 0.84 btc at Kraken

62124449
>>62123590
You have a point here tho. The use cases are still early, so skepticism makes sense. What’s interesting is the idea of extending utility while keeping Bitcoin unchanged and kept in self custody.

62124459
>>62116911
>ETH is cool but why LINK?

62124487
2.3 BTC and 36 ETH.

62124590
>>62115225
Giga Chad, I kneel

62124643
>>62117007
His choice lol

62125521
>>62117185
$30k? Let's see if you're right.

62125560
1.4 BTC

It doesn't feel like enough. I'm 36 now, and my dream of living a wealthy and comfy life before the age of 40 is basically done for. Is it even possible to have a fun life at 50+? I really lament not getting into BTC back in 2014. It's not like I didn't know about it back then.

item.title: HoW iS eVerYonE dOinG this? — 104 replies — 53.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
HoW iS eVerYonE dOinG this?

Open thread • Replies: 104 • Updated: 2026-04-17 15:11 UTC

OP

I love watching normie carhag/carfag compilations where Normies complain about how everything is too expensive.

I live on ~24k a year in a HCOL city making $26/hr
>$820 rent utilities included
>free transportation (fare fags aren’t checking train during my commute hours)
>$300/mo groceries (steak and potatoes mostly)
>$60/mo unlimited data
>$15/mo rent insurance
>$30 dental insurance *shrugs*

I regularly spend $8 on coffee, $15 on cigs, $30 dollars on pizza, and I STILL usually dump minimum $1500 a month into my brokerage account. Next month my credit limit will go from 12k>24k when I open another chase CC. I have 6 months expenses in a savings account and the equivalent in dry powder in my brokerage.

Being completely unstressed/concerned about money and whipping out my girthy 12k CL sapphire preferred card on cutie coffee sluts 4 times a week to order my $9 dollar 24oz drum of double chocolate mocha and then ripping a Newport 100 on a comfy bench while i enjoy my coffee is all the pleasure I need in this world

Early replies (first 3)

62107190
It's funny because I'm supposed to be an Europoor but my life standars is better than 99% of Americans lmfao.

I've never heard a single European complaining about debt. But you meet one of those Amerimutts that come to our continent for tourism or to teach English, and 5 minutes into the conversation they will start telling how how much in debt they are. BAFFLING.

62107202
>>62107169
>HCOL city
>$820 rent utilities included
and then your dad stopped molesting you and the out of body disassociation cope ended.

62107317
>>62107169
>>62107190
I'm American. Envious, ungrateful, whiny, bitchy Americans are an embarrassment. It's really not hard to live here at all. The problem is almost every retard that lives here thinks you should be able to afford a 2,300 sqft house on 1 acre of land, while having a new Toyota Camry in the driveway and enough in savings to go on 2 - 3 week-long vacations a year after putting 15% into an investment account, all on a McDonald's wage, or a basic-bitch corporate cubicle job, while only working 30 hours a week (yes, even 40 hours is too much work for Americans now).

Most people live WAY above their means. It's just a mixture of low-IQ combined with a set of personality traits that make them susceptible to short-term thinking and hedonism. Then they beg for free-gibs from the government while producing less material goods and services. It doesn't take a rocket scientist to figure out where this leads to.

I feel zero pity or sympathy for any of these people. Fuck them and their retarded choices.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62119779
OP here, thanks to whoever revived my thread
>>62110818
Good luck family man
>>62111774
Correct bucko
>>62114448
>>62114452
Coping chimp alert
>>62114990
Nope, right here.

Rent is 820 and the rest is on the ole chase sapphire. So I can see exactly what I’m spending. Don’t go too crazy trying to hone in on any particular number for my coffee budget or cigs etc. the bottom line is I spend about 2k a month on everything incl. rent.

It’s a 120sqft “””efficiency””” studio that’s why the rent is so cheap in this HCOL city.

62119791
>>62119779
>Rent is 820
>It’s a 120sqft “””efficiency””” studio
WTF? I mortgage a 1,000sqft house for $1,100/month.

62119962
>>62119791
You probably live in Ohio or one of those “fucking nowhere places”

I live in a city where farting in wine glasses and whiffing it would seem normal

62119967
>>62119962
>I live in a city where farting in wine glasses and whiffing it would seem normal
Skill issue

62120037
>>62119962
>I live in a city where farting in wine glasses and whiffing it would seem normal
New Delhi?

62120104
>>62119967
>>62120037
Alright ALRIGHT fellas listen. Its seattle. Yes, that’s right. I’m rubbing shoulders with tech dorks from Microsoft AND google.

I bet some of them don’t even have a sapphire preferred

62120111
>>62107169
>12k CL sapphire preferred card
Jeets think a 12k credit limit is alot. Nigga, I OWE that on just one of my cards.

62120298
>>62120111
NO WAY WHAT
SHE REALLY IS A HE

62120353
Saving and investing while living with the rents works. Im 34 and I’m damn nearly a millionaire and all I’ve done is be a zogbot and work gov or gov contractor jobs, but I have more liquid assets than friends who went into tech that inflated their COL

62120364
>>62111774
Well then clearly he doesn't live off 24k a year since his expenses alone are more than that. After taxes he's bringing in probably around 48k, and his margin of error for spending is super low. I highly doubt he's being real as a smoking habit would easily eat into any excess funds he has.

>>62119779
>120 sq ft
Ah I see, your memeing that you own nothing and are happy. Makes sense

62120406
>>62112085
Are you old and retarded? You can get a smartphone for like 300 bucks that will last 5 years. And jobs expect you to have them. Your bank uses apps, your job will use a app for timekeeping, you will get emails with paperwork to fill out before end of the day with a digital signature. It is a source of music vs cds dvds and a player, it gets rid of the maps on your car you used to have to buy, you don't need to buy a watch, etc.... literally for 300 bucks. For a device that your will be paying the bulk of the monthly for just a basic phone. Unlimited for me is 35$/month. 3 gigs was like 25$ which is the plan i would use if i had a flip phone. Ffs you can get a free smartphone if you are on ebt. If people took your advice, they would probably end up with shitty assets and the inability to be hired anywhere but starbucks or under the table garbage.

62121038
>>62120406
> Your bank uses apps
Banks do have apps, but you can still do all your banking on a computer.
> your job will use a app for timekeeping, you will get emails with paperwork to fill out before end of the day with a digital signature.
Not my problem. I don't work a wagie job.
> It is a source of music vs cds dvds and a player
Why pay subscription fees for music when I can load up an old iPod with all the music I want and can listen to it all without an internet connection
> it gets rid of the maps on your car you used to have to buy
GPS devices for cars exist. They're often more accurate and lower cost.
> you don't need to buy a watch
Watches are based. Only Zoomers and thirdies think otherwise.
> If people took your advice, they would probably end up with shitty assets and the inability to be hired anywhere but starbucks or under the table garbage.
Ramblings of an illiterate, brain-dead Zoomer who can't imagine life without eight hours of daily screen time on his phone.

62121080
>>62107387
Fucking LARP
I literally live right off MLK way in Seattle and one bed studios start at like $1200 a month. I live in a 2 bedroom that costs me $1900/month before utilities
Either you and OP are bullshitting or you have no conception of what a HCOL city really is

62121133
>>62121080
he's LARPing. you can't even get a studio in a nigger shithole like camden, nj for under $1k a month.

62123272
>>62107190
Yeah but what kind of car do you drive? How is your garbage disposal? Is your refrigerator taller than you are? Do you have a laundry room with washer and dryer?

Americans are retarded but earn more and buy more to debt hard.

62123382
>>62120353
I lived with my father until he died two years ago and now live by myself in the house. it definitely helped me save a lot. I'm 42 and between a taxable investment account, 401k, and cash I am around 1.5. the problem is you have to have no social life for this strategy to work long term. I would trade it all away in exchange for having had a normal social life and a wife, kids, etc.

62123416
>>62120353
Woa man you’re doing great.
>>62121080
Cope I can’t believe a fellow seattleanon would do me like that. You’re making me feel smug as fuck. There are no lies. It’s not a standard studio you dingbat. No stove, no bathtub, yet I’m grateful. Airfrying Chuck steaks and microwaving greens in my silicon steamer thing.

I’m living large pal.
>>62121133
*smuggies multiply*

Don’t seethe guys. I legit found my living situation on Reddit.

Oh and anons? I don’t pay for the 2 line to get to and from work :) I’m actually riding it now. We at Justin’s park
>>62123382
You could still get a SEAbunny wife

62123420
>>62107169
> I live on ~24k a year in a HCOL city making $26/hr
That’s not HCOL lol
HCOL would require $150k plus

62123428
>>62123420
Cope. Only Normie retards need 150k. I’m a friendless loser

62123431
>>62123428
You just said you live in hcol. Being able to survive on 24k means youre not in a hcol area.

62123432
>>62123428
I don’t think you know what hcol or vhcol is. I live in a vhcol area. I don’t need to cope.

62123434
>>62123428
This isn’t about normie or friends. We are clearly focusing on hcol or not.

62123439
>>62123431
>>62123432
>>62123434
Ok ferrets, yes or no: Seattle is a HCOL city. It’s regularly in the top 10

I’m gonna need all of you to go fuck yourselves

62123446
>>62123439
WHERE in Seattle. The ghetto areas?
Each city had vhcol areas then hcol then mcol and etc.
24k is lower class income. And that anon is saying he lives in hcol area. He probably lives in a lcol area in an expensive city. It is like how even LA or nyc had cheap areas.
Like come on man. You’re arguing with me when that anon already said he makes 24k.

62123451
>>62123439
Seattle? Sure, depends on neighborhood. Tf is wrong with you? Youre poor and so desperate to say you live in “muh hcol” city.

62123458
>>62123439
This board is like 10% business discussion contributed by 10% of its posters.
I can’t believe youre even arguing with me AFTER the guy said he makes 24k. Lmao. You make yourself look so stupid and this userbase.

62123461
>>62107169
>newport
are you even white? wtf are you doing on this website. gtfo.

62123464
Your city might have “expensive city” reputation but you clearly live in a cheap area with ghetto people. Assuming you didn’t make a troll thread.

62123466
>>62107190
America is a debt based economy.
You are a cuck based economy.

62125196
>>62123451
Oh be fucking quiet trying to limp along with your shitty point by partitioning up my city into neighborhoods. Holy shit. It’s not even that big. The blacks live in Kent and Tacoma so don’t even start.
>>62123464
No I don’t dumbass I can walk 15 minutes and I’m downtown

item.title: Why aren’t you holding BTC? — 109 replies — 51.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren’t you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 109 • Updated: 2026-04-17 14:59 UTC

OP

If you’re still watching or waiting to buy BTC, why not do it now?
>I guess you’re wasting time, or maybe you just prefer altcoins. What’s your reason?

Early replies (first 3)

62087977
>>62087937
I only watch it to laugh at Saylor and you bitsteinbaggies. Not buying digital nothing.

62087993
>>62087937
Waiting for sub60K

62087997
>>62087937
I don't have enough money for it to make me rich. Alts have a good chance of vastly outperforming BTC during the next big pump. I've decided to take the potential for a 10x over the guarantee of a 2x.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62106279
>>62106216
Its quite obvious desu

62106298
btc is going to 20k if not lower

>>62097398
>'good alts' will vastly outperform
have you been asleep for 5 years?

there was like 3 alts that outperformed btc out of literally millions. your odds are terrible for alts, much better chance to just throw it all on red and call it a day

62106312
>>62106298
>btc is going to 20k if not lower
>there was like 3 alts that outperformed btc out of literally millions
Bottom signal, the retards maxxing are back

62106435
>>62106298
Idiot

62106463
>>62097125
DCA doesn't work you stupid nigger. Just look at Michael Saylor, he's down -5% after SIX YEARS of dollar cost averaging

62107082
>>62106463

ngmi

You're not smart enough.

62108172
>>62095759
Why do you say that?

62109768
>>62097398
Sure, chase “good alts”… just don’t cry in a few years when they roundtrip to zero and BTC is sitting way higher.

You’ll wish you just held the hardest asset instead of playing ETF cosplay.

62110223
Guarantee 30% return on semiconductors and useless AI tech or gamble that Bitcoin crawls back to 100k any time soon despite losing 40% of it's value in the last few months. Hmmmm

62110242
I expected that until December Bitcoin will sunk to below 40k and Solana will be around $20. I will buy solana and Hold it until 2029 when the next bullrun will start.

2027-2028 will be crab market, everyone will say that crypto is dead just like 2023.

I will buy SOL because it will probably 20x while BTC will only 5x.

62110338
>>62102233
A 17 year old in the prime of their life.

62110351
>>62110338
Fact, can't wait to back kick some zombies

62110380
>>62087937
Why aren't you holding nano?

It literally has perfected digital money.

>I guess you’re wasting time, or maybe you just prefer inferior coins. What’s your reason?

62112997
>>62110380
Nano? Any major development?

62114412
>>62110351
What do you mean?

62114414
>>62110242
You're bearish man

62114891
>>62110223
Well, you should choose your preference

62114897
>>62106463
He's thinking long term tho

62117929
>>62097125
The just the best move, while also staking on TBVs instead of holding idle

62117990
Bitcoin is the Pedo coin

62119044
>>62117990
Fuddd

62123333
>>62093341
check clearpool for long, kusama for short term

62123643
>>62088069
I love these seething retards hahaha

62123772
>>62123333
just started, don't sell now

62124465
>>62097429
No way

62124708
>>62123333
next leg in 2 hours

62124800
I don't see the point now
I'm not investing for longevity and it's reached a plateau

Would have been worth it years ago but I had no money and couldn't convince my retard family to invest

Id rather gamble on altcoins with 10x potential

62124932
>>62087937

62125025
>>62124932
Ah gold
>useless in socialist managed decline as you will not retain asset value when selling, if they don't just confiscate it
>useless in societal collapse as soon as the new civilisation is formed because they will just take it and you won't have more guns or dudes to stop this
>price fixing to discourage investing in, and ensure gouging when consumers get wise
It's a shit asset brother

62125120
>>62117990
Pedo's run the world. Bullish for BTC

item.title: Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition — 57 replies — 46.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-04-17 15:54 UTC

OP

Hello my fellow PolkaCurry brothers. We are again here, for another thread where we discuss everything related to DOT, Mumbai (the city of DOT), curry and more. The last thread was a resounding success, we managed to keep it alive for literally 41 days. We got paid 11500 + 5*150 = 12250 DOT for the thread and are now living in a period of enlightenment while eating with METAL spoons. Brothers, we have truly reached enlightenment. WE, the Mumbai Warriors will strike yet again and collect generations worth of money !

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Early replies (first 3)

62115437
We are eating with our METAL spoons in Mumbai. No more hands my brethren. We eat with out spoons, like proud warriors.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62115624
>>62115424
>>62115437

Still gonna larp as an Indian?

62115793
>>62115437

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62118020
>>62116823
Picrel

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62118038
>>62116823
Picrel

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62118201
>>62116823
Another thing that might interest you is this page: https://polkadot-fellows.github.io/RFCs/ . Polkadot uses standardized RFC proposals to build DOT out. You can see the new proposals here.
Here's the Parity roadmap: https://github.com/orgs/paritytech/projects/27/views/1

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62118271
>>62116823
>. I'm gonna do a bit of research into the polkadot team
The DOT team is literally Parity in a sense. Gavin left ETH to become the CEO of Parity and build DOT. He then became the main guy in DOT and left the role of CEO of Parity. Now he became the CEO back again and left DOT as the main guy in order to pivot Parity into building on top of DOT. The team that's working on DOT is called the technical fellowship. It consists of people who were mostly part of Parity. Parity literally built the BEST chain in the ecosystem and are now pivoting from building the chain into building the products that go along with it.
Read this: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/paritytech_the-best-way-to-improve-polkadot-is-to-build-activity-7388496946916712448-0a1n and https://x.com/alice_und_bob/status/1955655592191619307 and https://www.bitget.com/news/detail/12560605074159

They were building DOT for 10 years, now the focus shifts to building products on top of DOT for the next 10 years. Of course JAM and NOMT are still releasing this year. Both combined with every other previous update will scale the network to millions of TPS, maybe 10s of millions TPS. The chain will be years ahead. It already is. One core will be able to handle more TPS than most other chains can handle on the whole chain. You already have cheap transactions and they're going to get even cheaper and faster (Due to 500ms block, Basti blocks) and there is NO cutoff when it comes to decentralization (SOL broke down 8 or 10 times was it?). No other chain compares to DOT even remotely.

62118281
>>62116823
>. I'm gonna do a bit of research into the polkadot team
The DOT team is literally Parity in a sense. Gavin left ETH to become the CEO of Parity and build DOT. He then became the main guy in DOT and left the role of CEO of Parity. Now he became the CEO back again and left DOT as the main guy in order to pivot Parity into building on top of DOT. The team that's working on DOT is called the technical fellowship. It consists of people who were mostly part of Parity. Parity literally built the BEST chain in the ecosystem and are now pivoting from building the chain into building the products that go along with it.
Read this: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/paritytech_the-best-way-to-improve-polkadot-is-to-build-activity-7388496946916712448-0a1n and https://x.com/alice_und_bob/status/1955655592191619307 and https://www.bitget.com/news/detail/12560605074159

They were building DOT for 10 years, now the focus shifts to building products on top of DOT for the next 10 years. Of course JAM and NOMT are still releasing this year. Both combined with every other previous update will scale the network to millions of TPS, maybe 10s of millions TPS. The chain will be years ahead. It already is. One core will be able to handle more TPS than most other chains can handle on the whole chain. You already have cheap transactions and they're going to get even cheaper and faster (Due to 500ms block, Basti blocks) and there is NO cutoff when it comes to decentralization (SOL broke down 8 or 10 times was it?). No other chain compares to DOT even remotely.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62118362
How much of this gay token do I need to buy to become a rich chad man in my next life and fuck stacies all day?

62118622
>>62118362
>How much of this gay token do I need to buy to become a rich chad man in my next life and fuck stacies all day?
0. You can already do all of that. If you don't believe me then ask yourself this question "Are there people who became rich without buying DOT?" then ask yourself this question "Do people fuck badies all day long without DOT?".

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62119101
>>62118271
The team needs to move faster

62119758
100$ end of bull run. At the end of the bull run the Mumbai warriors are going to eat with GOLDEN spoons I tell you. Right now we're in the metal era. Sooner or later we'll be at the GOLDEN era. You guys have no clue what's going to happen. We are THE CHAIN. Everyone is going to try to pick up our poops after we finish right into the street. Our poops will be sold on the internet for everyone will know that the Mumbai warriors manure is valuable. Once JAM launches the first use case will be to fully decentralize all the infrastructure of Polkadot, like the website https://polkadot.com/. Sooner or later all the infrastructure associated to Polkadot will be run on-chain. It's the best presentation of it simply working. Why use AWS Amazon if you can simply just host everything directly on DOT and therefore prove to the world that we're capable and ready for the masses?

We shall rise up my Mumbai warriors into a new dawn!

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62121057
>>62119758
Compete against Amazon?

Polkadot better bring any impressive presentation

62121440
>>62121057
>Polkadot better bring any impressive presentation
You do realize that AWS fails multiple times per year and with that it takes down like 50% of the internet? DOT has a 100% uptime. Just simply that one fact makes it superior.

https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2041480013724135847

Having the internet hosted on centralized servers makes no sense. Imagine smart cities hosted on centralized servers and the servers going down. A whole city would simply halt. The reality is that we can't afford down times. We need Web3 cloud to provide a 100% up time. Centralized server simply DO NOT cut it. Every time AWS goes down literally 50% of the internet simply does not work. That's lowkey cooked.

62121668
>>62121440
Great. Name recognition reigns supreme here. Plus Amazon has billions of dollars to address their flaws

62121692
75$ in two years and 5 months

62121723
>>62121668
>Name recognition reigns supreme here
That's great that you pointed that out and at the same time every company sooner or later goes under. There does not exist a company in history of mankind that survived the test of time. No name was ever big enough to sustain itself through time. The only thing that never changes is the fact that everything always changes.

>Plus Amazon has billions of dollars to address their flaws
That's true and the thing is that no amount of money can solve centralized server uptime. There's always something. If you have a system as complex as AWS that's run across multiple countries and relies of tens if not hundreds of millions of lines of code then there's ALWAYS going to be a flaw. It's impossible to have a system as big as AWS, that runs of centralized servers, and that it works all the time. No matter how much money they spent, no matter how many smart engineers they throw at it... The only solution is a Web3 cloud. Can Amazon pivot into a Web3 cloud? Sure. They can literally just use DOT as the underlying infrastructure. DOT is NOT against Amazon. It can compliment it. Amazon can just simply bend the knee and use DOT as their compute provider, right? If Amazon doesn't follow the trend then sooner or later people will abandon it. Why would you use a more expensive cloud provider that has less up time? There is NO point. I as a CS myself know this for a fact. You earlier said
>Name recognition reigns supreme here
To be honest? If I can get a cloud provider for cheaper and have them have more uptime I'd switch in a heartbeat. The same rules don't apply in computers/programming than they do in other sectors. People who are into computers move faster than other sectors, because we're taught BY DEFAULT that things constantly change and never stay the same. If you're paying 5% more and the uptime is 5% less then you're literally shooting yourself in the foot.

62121726
75$ end of race

62121749
>>62121668
What I'm trying to say is that CS is the fastest moving sector for a reason. And the reason is that people in the sector love adopting new technology at a fast pace. If I can get something for 5% cheaper with 5% more uptime and I have the time to migrate my service and it's not too much of a hastle then I'd do it in a heartbeat. Sure if the migration would be EXTREMELY TEDIOUS then I might reconsider. It's just, normally it's not and takes maybe a day or two. Imagine your whole company paying 5% less while your customers would be 5% happier with your service. You'd be stupid not to take that deal, right? Centralized servers ARE EXPENSIVE in nature. Decentralized servers are INEXPENSIVE. Why? Well centralized stuff needs upkeep while decentralized stuff does not. Look at Acurast (https://acurast.com/). They're literally a mobile phone cloud provider. How much does the upkeep for your mobile phone cost? 0$. Do you need janitors and cleaning personnel? Nope. Do you need engineers to run your phone? Nope. Do you see what I mean? The price margin for 1GHz of computing power provided by a mobile device is probably 100x lower than 1GHz of computing power provided by a centralized server. You would switch cloud providers if they offered a 100x lower price while providing a 100% uptime, right? You wouldn't be like "Oh, but their NAME is not AMAZON. OMG OMG. Let's not use this because they have no NAME RECOGNITION. I'd rather pay 100x more and have less uptime than switch. OMG OMG OMG". Do you notice how that sounds once I put it into perspective? Sounds awkward, right?

>>62121692
>>62121726
Soon we shall catapult into the heavens of nirvana.

62121769
>>62121723
> every company goes under
Not quite true. Sure majority do go under, but successful companies adapt to economic changes, merge, or subsidize
Amazon created quite the monopoly in the retail sector. Hard to envision a downturn anytime soon.

>AWS pivots to Web3 cloud
Point taken. There again will DOT have a successful launch once all its compon8 are built in? Will it have enough marketing? Will a new challenger approach?

62121787
>>62121749
Most people would agree Linux is the superior OS against Windows OS

Who still owns the OS space?

Exactly

62121817
>>62121787
>Most people would agree Linux is the superior OS against Windows OS
Great that you point that out. That's an apples to oranges comparison. Linux isn't meant for the average user. It's meant for programmers in a lot of ways. We're also not talking about OS. Are we?

>>62121769
>Not quite true. Sure majority do go under, but successful companies adapt to economic changes, merge, or subsidize
True, and no company survives in it's original form. Everyone adapts to the time. And the time is shifting towards a Web3 cloud.

>Hard to envision a downturn anytime soon.
True, and I'm not saying that. What I'm saying is that Web3 will, over time, take a large share away from it, due to it being cheaper and having more uptime. Again, centralized servers CAN NOT compete with decentralized ones on uptime and cost. And those two factors are literally the only two important factors when it comes to a cloud provider. It has to be cheap and have the largest possible uptime.

>Point taken. There again will DOT have a successful launch once all its compon8 are built in? Will it have enough marketing? Will a new challenger approach?
The thing is that Amazon will just use Web3 clouds. I doubt they'll build their own because they'd have to spent a lot of money to re-invent the wheel. DOT is already ready in a lot of ways. This year JAM hits and that'll transform it into a TRUE Web3 cloud capable of handling the load of AWS. Look, I'm not saying that DOT will IMMEDIATELY kill everyone. It'll be a slow transition and more and more companies will be switching. You know the 20/80 rule? It takes as much time to go from 0% to 20% than it does from 20% to 80%. This was true for smart phones, the internet, cars, ... So, if it took 10 years to go from 0% users to 20% of the population using something then it'll take that same amount of time to go from 20% to 80%. TO BE CONTINUED

62121830
>>62121769
Copied from Google AI

As of early 2026, over 650 million to nearly 1 billion people worldwide are estimated to own or use cryptocurrency. Growth is driven by high adoption in regions like Asia and Africa, with about 6.8% of the global population holding digital assets. While ownership is high, active monthly users are estimated lower, around 30–60 million, indicating a large population of passive holders.

Projected Growth: The number of users is expected to reach 993 million by 2026, with user penetration expected to reach 12.63% of the population, according to 1.
Regional Leaders: Asia dominates with 43% of global users, followed by North America (17%) and Europe (15%).
Top Adoption Countries: India, Nigeria, and Indonesia show high adoption rates, often driven by local currency instability, according to 4 and 3.
User Demographics: Approximately 60% of crypto users are under the age of 34, with a majority being male.

Active on-chain usage continues to rise as mobile wallet installations grow, with over 1.1 billion installations tracked as of early 2026.


We're almost at the 20% mark. BTC was created 17 years ago. So, we'll be at the 20% mark by like 2028 to 2030. So like 19 to 21 years. So, by the year 2050 80% of the world population will be using Web3.

62121862
I'm not sure if you guys are looking at the chart but we're really close to breaking out from the bottom
Look at picrel >>62118020 and >>62118038

62121888
>>62121769
Will it have enough marketing?
No worries my brother. I have made a marketing contract with Jeny Smith (https://thothub.mx/videos/1505023/jeny-smith-polka-dot-pantyhose/). She will market DOT for us. We are set my Mumbai brother

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62121922
>>62121862
I'm not sure if you guys noticed. It's an adam and eve pattern on the 4h chart. Look at picrel

62121953
>>62121862
>>62121922

62122759
>>62121922
>>62121953

Chart looks good

But in the last thread weren't you shorting DOT

62123242
>>62122759
Panjali, be quite you fool (https://boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/61932834#p62069282). Do you want the Mumbai warriors to eat with their hands again? Or do you want to upgrade from our metal spoons to silver and then golden spoons? We, the Mumbai warriors, are painting the graph bullish in order to lure people in, then we strike with a x125 short. We actually been holding short open since 55$. The whole of Mumbai will eat from that singular short. Panjali, you have much to learn about the Vishnu ways. It's ok my son. I forgive you. Together we are strong, divided we are weak.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62124594
>>62123242
Way to promote a project then rug people

62125019
Didint read thread, i Will let My id decide if i buy some DOT when i get home

62125037
>>62125019
w4, E3, Wasb, and purple colour... Yes.. i see...

Not buying.

62125463
this is the most demoralizing dot thread

item.title: Why is everyone so fucking retarded about AI? — 21 replies — 16.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is everyone so fucking retarded about AI?

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-04-17 15:52 UTC

OP

Why is everyone so fucking retarded about AI? Its making everyone more productive. There are going to be more jobs, not less. JFQ

Early replies (first 3)

62121855
>>62121839
Try persuading that to a pretentious artfag

62121857
>>62121839
yes, that's why paypal fired almost half its staff - the AI use is going to make companies want to hire more people :^)

62121868
>>62121857

Companies are absolutely 100% rehiring engineers they laid off. Its not sexy so it doesn't fit the narrative or make the news.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62121875
>>62121857
jobs change all the time. no one works as a typewriter repair person anymore. its not a tragedy.

62121943
>>62121875
this
it's far far away from being totally autonomous.
The language learning process is unable to make the
next cognitive step.
We have seen the many speech fail clips
across all ai platforms.

Devs know this barrier but don't talk about it much.

62121965
AI is great for sigma males who have vision. I saved hundreds of thousands building my business. No need for devs, CPAs, Lawyers, Marketing Agencies, etc.

62121969
cool story bro

62121987
>>62121857
Companies are using AI as a scapegoat for firing. In reality AI doesn't matter to the job count at all

62122155
>>62121839
>There are going to be more jobs, not less.
This is what they tell the goyim kek
In the end the welfare class will be much larger and those who work will be competing with machines that just keep getting better.
The ones who win will be the shareholders of the companies that win the great AI race. Invest wisely.

62122575
>>62122155
And this is what the baghokders telling the goyim(ticker:buy my bags)

62122602
>>62122155
This shilling is the reason for the hype and scaremongering. Not buying, overvalued

62122961
>>62121839
you dont get it anon, its not that there willbe more or less jobs. Think of it this way: Those who adopt AI to learn from and become greater will stand out. Those who use AI to think for them will become brainlets and those who are afraid of AI and do not adapt it will fall off, die and be left behind. This is a filter event and right now people are completely complacent of the actual reality going on. I use AI to enhance and build upon my skills. In fact it removes most middle men and lets me directly solve issues with my clientele. Which are you anon? Are you evolving or becoming a part of the past?

62123889
>>62121839
It's a grift. That's it. You remember fidget spinners? Same thing. The only difference was when fidget spinners were no longer popular you STILL HAD A FUCKING FIDGET SPINNER.

62123898
>>62121857
>>62121868
They ARE rehiring engineers, just those engineers are working from India and being paid 20% the previous engineers' salary.
AI = Actually Indians

62123902
>>62123898
Actually, they are also onshoring a lot of them, meaning they're bringing them to western countries on work visas, and they're able to pay them less and treat them like shit because their residence is tied to their job.

62123906
>>62121987
This

62124127
>>62122961
>>62121965
I don't believe this shit, all the models still get basic shit wrong about finance when I ask it. If your trusting your business to AI your either retarded and will get sued, or you don't make any money and are larping.

62124442
>>62121857
16% over two years is not half you fucking retard

62125149
>>62121839
probably because it's financially unsustainable and 90% hype

62125178
>they forgot to program the computers not to lie

top kek

62125452
AI is an iq test. If you fall for the “ai is evil, ai doesn’t actually work” scam, you’re going to be part of the next gen of tech illiterate old people.

item.title: XRP – Inverted Reality($17, $27, $35 & $42): Flip the chart… and everything changes. — 5 replies — 14.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP – Inverted Reality($17, $27, $35 & $42): Flip the chart… and everything changes.

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-04-17 16:47 UTC

OP

XRP – Inverted Reality($17, $27, $35 & $42):

Flip the chart… and everything changes.

I’ve done the inversion, now it’s on you to define the targets.

Are you seeing structure… or still trapped in noise?

Structure > Noise

ONLY FEW UNDERSTAND

Early replies (first 3)

62123899
KABOOOOOOOM

62125564
>>62123851
Wow, if you flip the chart on btc it looks like a line gradually going down
If you flip it on shitcoins it still looks like insider trading

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62125650
>>62123851

62125768
>>62123851
I see you bagholders have started RETARDMAXXING.

item.title: BAT Thread — 13 replies — 11.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
BAT Thread

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-04-17 16:00 UTC

OP

Are you still there BAT bros? OGs report in. Does anyone still have faith that we will make it?

Early replies (first 3)

62121824
>>62121811
Yikes I haven’t held bat since 2019

62121956
>>62121824
Come home white man. Never a better time to get back in

62122071
Is thotposter still around?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62122103
>>62121956
>white man
>coin entirely dominated by jeets

62122112
>>62121811
Fuck this token. Uphold Wallet is ass!

62122439
>>62122071
>thotposter
Who? This board is hella dead. Actually surprised to see a BAT thread again.

>>62122112
Self Serve has been out for a minute, lad.

62122502
>>62121811
I don't have the image on hand but apparently Kevin Warsh, the incoming fedboi, has lots of crypto, notably SOL, COMP, and BAT lol

62122525
>>62122439
Is the self serve ad db finally out and they only needed 6 fucking years for that? Wow!

62122714
>>62122439
how can men be gay?

62122750
>>62122525
If we're lucky, Brave Search won't fucking suck 6 dicks in a row in 2032.

>>62122714
Good question, lad.

62124615
>>62122750
Why the fuck do Jannie’s exist? Anyone got a copy?

62125490
>>62121811
Makes me sad every time I think about BAT. This time it might be dead for real. Will be lucky to break 50 cents next bull run, let alone 1 dollar

62125502
>>62124615
https://i.warosu.org/data/biz/img/0621/21/1776382087181718.webm
https://i.warosu.org/data/biz/img/0621/21/1776375494543689.webm

>>62122439
